title: Game Feel — Haptics, Animations & Visual Feedback
problem: Combat, level-ups, and loot drops are all text-only events with no physical or visual feedback. Hits, crits, defeats, and victories all feel the same. There are no screen shake animations, no HP bar flash on damage taken, no haptic pulses, no level-up overlay. The gap between mechanics and feel makes the game feel like a spreadsheet rather than an action RPG.
analysis: CombatScreen uses static Text/View components with no animation. React Native Animated API is available with no new packages. expo-haptics is included in Expo SDK and just needs an import. Combat log is a plain list of grey strings. Level-up is only visible in the combat log text. GridScreen tile taps have no feedback. The gap between a crit doing 80 damage and a normal hit doing 10 is invisible — both look identical in the UI.
implementation: 1. src/hooks/useHaptics.ts — wraps expo-haptics: impactLight, impactMedium, impactHeavy, notificationSuccess, notificationError. Guards against haptics disabled in settingsStore. 2. src/hooks/useCombatAnimations.ts — returns Animated.Value instances: shakeX (translateX for player HP bar), monsterFlash (opacity/color for monster HP bar), levelUpPulse (scale for level-up overlay). triggerShake(): Animated.sequence([...3 oscillations...]).start(). triggerFlash(): Animated sequence on monster bar. 3. CombatScreen.tsx — wrap player HP bar in Animated.View with translateX from shakeX. Wrap monster HP bar section in Animated.View with opacity flash. In attackAction callback: trigger monsterFlash. When playerHp decreases: trigger shakeX + impactMedium. On crit in log: impactHeavy. On victory: notificationSuccess. On defeat: notificationError. On potion: impactLight. 4. src/components/LevelUpOverlay.tsx — full-screen semi-transparent gold overlay. Shows '★ LEVEL UP ★', new level number, stat gains. useEffect watches gameStore.level — when it increases, animate in (scale 0→1, opacity 0→1) then auto-dismiss after 1.8s. 5. Combat log color coding: lines containing 'crit'/'CRIT' → #f39c12 orange, 'Victory'/'XP' → #2ecc71 green, 'damage'/'dealt' → #e74c3c red, 'healed'/'Potion'→ #27ae60, 'Scroll'→ #9b59b6, 'LEVEL UP'→ #c8a020 gold, default → #666 grey. 6. GridScreen.tsx — tile onPress: impactLight. Encounter discovery (boss/rare/chest): impactHeavy. 7. All haptic calls gated by settingsStore.hapticsEnabled (read from store directly, no prop drilling).

decisions.decision: Haptics via expo-haptics with silent fallback for devices without support
decisions.rationale: expo-haptics is already in the Expo SDK; the try/catch silent fallback pattern means haptics degrade gracefully on simulators and older devices.

decisions.decision: Log entry types drive color — crit gold, xp teal, scroll purple, victory green, levelUp gold, heal green, enraged orange
decisions.rationale: Typed log entries decouple color from content string parsing; adding a new event type only requires adding to the union.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: tier background tints — TIER_BG map with 5 subtle hues per tier applied to gridArea background
decisions.rationale: Subtle tints signal depth tier change without being distracting; pure black for all tiers made every tier feel identical.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: per-item quality loot log in handleVictory — star unique, diamond rare, diamond magic, dot common — each with typed log color
decisions.rationale: Previously all loot dropped as a single summary line. Per-item lines with quality prefixes let players see at a glance what they found without leaving the combat log.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: monster affix names resolved from MONSTER_AFFIXES map in combat-start log at enraged type
decisions.rationale: Raw affix IDs like fireEnchanted were logged previously. Human-readable names from MONSTER_AFFIXES make the log immediately legible.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: flee button label changed to stays/blocked to clarify that fleeing keeps the player on the current floor
decisions.rationale: Players assumed flee meant game over or retreat to town. The label stays makes clear the cost is staying on the floor, not losing progress.

successCriteria: Combat hit triggers haptic. Player HP bar visibly shakes on damage received. Monster HP bar flashes red when damaged. Level-up overlay appears and dismisses after 1.8s. Combat log lines have distinct colors by event type. Grid tile taps have haptic response.
knownLimitations: No particle effects (would require a dedicated library like react-native-skia)
Shake animation is horizontal only — vertical shake not implemented
LevelUpOverlay only triggers mid-combat — level-up during town/menu transitions won't show it (rare edge case)
userGuide: Game feel is transparent to the player — you just feel it. Hits shake the screen. Crits hit harder. Level-ups are celebrated. If haptics feel too intense, toggle them off in Settings.
